title: andropogon scoparius subsp. genuinus
intro: Andropogon scoparius subsp. genuinus, also known as broom sedge, is a type of plant native to North America, characterized by its sharp, pointed leaves and feathery plumes. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities. The health benefits of broom sedge include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as treating wounds and skin irritations due to its antiseptic properties. Broom sedge can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs in herbal preparations to enhance its medicinal effects.
